message:
feat(fnx-algorithms,conformance): expand algorithms and add adversarial tiebreak corpus

- Expand fnx-algorithms graph implementations (+85/-7 lines)
- Add cgse_adversarial_tiebreak_corpus_v1.json adversarial fixture
- Add cgse_adversarial_tiebreak_corpus.rs test module
- Update Cargo.lock and beads issue tracker

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -191,7 +191,7 @@
 {"id":"franken_networkx-hl4","title":"Add doc comments to all epsilon/threshold constants in fnx-algorithms","description":"## What

Add explanatory doc comments to all named constants at the top of
crates/fnx-algorithms/src/lib.rs (lines 15-27) explaining:
- What each constant controls
- Why this specific value was chosen
- What NetworkX does for comparison
- What happens if the value is too large or too small

## Current Constants

```rust
const PAGERANK_DEFAULT_ALPHA: f64 = 0.85;          // NX default is also 0.85
const PAGERANK_DEFAULT_MAX_ITERATIONS: usize = 100; // NX default is also 100
const PAGERANK_DEFAULT_TOLERANCE: f64 = 1.0e-6;     // NX default is also 1e-6
const KATZ_DEFAULT_ALPHA: f64 = 0.1;                // NX default is also 0.1
const KATZ_DEFAULT_BETA: f64 = 1.0;                 // NX default is also 1.0
const KATZ_DEFAULT_MAX_ITERATIONS: usize = 1000;    // NX default is also 1000
const KATZ_DEFAULT_TOLERANCE: f64 = 1.0e-6;         // NX default is also 1e-6
const HITS_DEFAULT_MAX_ITERATIONS: usize = 100;     // NX default is also 100
const HITS_DEFAULT_TOLERANCE: f64 = 1.0e-8;         // NX default is also 1e-8
const DISTANCE_COMPARISON_EPSILON: f64 = 1.0e-12;   // Our own threshold
const FLOW_EPSILON: f64 = 1.0e-10;                  // Our own threshold
const ASSORTATIVITY_EPSILON: f64 = 1.0e-15;         // Our own threshold
const PARAM_SUM_EPSILON: f64 = 1.0e-6;              // Same as NX
```

## Why This Matters

These constants define the numerical behavior of the entire algorithm library.
Without documentation, future contributors might:
- Change PAGERANK_DEFAULT_ALPHA without realizing it breaks NX parity
- Tighten FLOW_EPSILON to 1e-15 causing infinite loops in min_cost_flow
- Loosen DISTANCE_COMPARISON_EPSILON causing non-deterministic shortest paths

## Files To Change

- crates/fnx-algorithms/src/lib.rs (lines 15-27)

## Template

```rust
/// Damping factor for PageRank. NetworkX default: 0.85.
/// Higher values weight link structure more; lower values bias toward uniform.
/// Must match NX for parity — do not change without updating conformance fixtures.
const PAGERANK_DEFAULT_ALPHA: f64 = 0.85;
```","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-25T03:05:57.639838945Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-25T03:12:17.235104755Z","closed_at":"2026-03-25T03:12:17.234589159Z","close_reason":"Added comprehensive doc comments to all 13 constants in fnx-algorithms. Each documents: purpose, NX default match, what happens if changed, and why this specific value.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"franken_networkx-hnrr","title":"[B9] Cross-walk between Python parity tests and Rust conformance harness","description":"**Track:** B. **Priority:** P1. **Type:** task.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-09T00:23:26.167651233Z","updated_at":"2026-04-09T00:23:26.167651233Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"franken_networkx-hp3","title":"Flow Algorithms: min-cost flow, capacity scaling, network simplex","description":"## Scope
Implement 6 missing NetworkX flow algorithm functions:
- `min_cost_flow` — find minimum cost flow satisfying demands
- `min_cost_flow_cost` — return just the cost of the min-cost flow
- `max_flow_min_cost` — find max-flow, then minimize cost among all max-flows
- `cost_of_flow` — compute cost of a given flow dict
- `capacity_scaling` — successive shortest path with capacity scaling, O(V^2 * E * log(V*C))
- `network_simplex` — primal network simplex algorithm, O(V^3 * E)

## Algorithmic Approach
- capacity_scaling: Implement successive shortest path algorithm with scaling. Use Bellman-Ford for finding augmenting paths with negative costs. Scale capacities by powers of 2.
- network_simplex: Implement primal network simplex. Maintain spanning tree structure, find entering/leaving arcs via pricing.
- min_cost_flow / min_cost_flow_cost / max_flow_min_cost: Thin wrappers that call capacity_scaling or network_simplex with appropriate parameters.
- cost_of_flow: Simple summation over edges.

## Implementation Location
- Rust core: `crates/fnx-algorithms/src/flow/` — new module for min-cost flow
- Python bindings: `python/franken_networkx/algorithms/flow/`

## Edge Cases
- Negative cost cycles (must detect and handle)
- Infeasible flows (demand exceeds capacity, disconnected networks)
- Zero-capacity edges
- Self-loops
- Multigraph support (sum parallel edge capacities)
- Integer vs float costs/capacities

## Test Requirements
- Cross-validate against `networkx.algorithms.flow` oracle
- Test infeasible flow detection
- Test negative cycle detection
- Performance benchmarks: capacity_scaling on graphs with 10K+ nodes
- Property tests: flow conservation, capacity constraints, optimality conditions (reduced cost optimality)","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-21T19:10:59.587991045Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-22T03:18:05.448863036Z","closed_at":"2026-03-22T03:18:05.448305751Z","close_reason":"Implemented 7 flow functions: cost_of_flow, min_cost_flow (successive shortest paths with Bellman-Ford on residual graph), min_cost_flow_cost, max_flow_min_cost, capacity_scaling, network_simplex, flow_hierarchy. Unblocks connectivity and utilities beads. Total: 462 functions.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["algorithms","flow","tier-1"]}

 {"id":"franken_networkx-i1d","title":"Utility Extras B: remove attrs, describe, Floyd-Warshall numpy, misc","description":"Implement ~10 utility functions: remove_node_attributes(G, name) — delete attribute from all nodes; remove_edge_attributes(G, name) — delete from all edges; describe(G) — detailed graph description string; floyd_warshall_numpy(G, weight) — Floyd-Warshall via numpy matrix operations O(n^3); global_parameters(G) — global graph parameters (diameter, girth, etc.); intersection_array(G) — intersection array for distance-regular graphs; harmonic_diameter(G) — harmonic mean of all distances; goldberg_radzik(G, source, weight) — shortest paths with negative weights, faster than Bellman-Ford; is_perfect_graph(G) — check if graph is perfect (Strong Perfect Graph Theorem); tutte_polynomial(G, x, y) — Tutte polynomial evaluation via deletion-contraction. Tests: Floyd-Warshall numpy matches Dijkstra all-pairs, remove_attrs actually removes, NX parity.","status":"closed","priority":4,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-22T17:34:39.170489516Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-22T22:38:06.630383101Z","closed_at":"2026-03-22T22:38:06.629748971Z","close_reason":"Implemented 6 utility functions: remove_node_attributes, remove_edge_attributes, floyd_warshall_numpy, harmonic_diameter, global_parameters, intersection_array.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["algorithms"]}

 {"id":"franken_networkx-xkr","title":"Generators: Tree/forest utilities (junction tree, join_trees, random unlabeled)","description":"## Scope
Implement 8 tree/forest generation and utility functions:
- junction_tree(G) — compute tree decomposition of G. If G is chordal, the junction tree has cliques as nodes, connected iff they share nodes. Uses maximum cardinality search to find perfect elimination ordering, then extracts clique tree. O(n+m) for chordal graphs.
- join_trees(T1, T2, root1=None, root2=None) — join two trees by adding an edge between root1 and root2. If roots not specified, use arbitrary nodes.
- random_unlabeled_tree(n, seed=None) — uniform random tree up to isomorphism. Uses Prüfer sequence generation then canonicalization.
- random_unlabeled_rooted_tree(n, seed=None) — rooted variant.
- random_unlabeled_rooted_forest(n, q=None, seed=None) — random rooted forest with parameter q controlling number of trees.
- tree_data(G, root, attrs=None) — serialize rooted tree to nested dict-of-dicts format {root: {child1: {...}, child2: {...}}}.
- tree_graph(data, attrs=None) — inverse of tree_data, reconstruct tree from nested dict.
- complete_to_chordal_graph(G) — add minimum fill edges to make G chordal. Uses elimination ordering heuristic (minimum degree or minimum fill). Returns (chordal_graph, added_edges).

## Implementation Strategy
- junction_tree: MCS → PEO → extract maximal cliques → build clique graph → compute MST of clique graph
- random_unlabeled: generate labeled tree, compute canonical form via AHU algorithm
- tree_data/tree_graph: recursive DFS serialization/deserialization
- complete_to_chordal: iteratively eliminate minimum-degree node, adding fill edges to make neighborhood a clique

## Test Requirements
- junction_tree: verify tree structure, verify clique intersection property
- tree_data(tree_graph(data)) == data for round-trip
- complete_to_chordal: result passes is_chordal(), added edges are minimal
- random_unlabeled: output is a tree (n-1 edges, connected)
- NX cross-validation on chordal and non-chordal graphs","status":"closed","priority":4,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-22T17:34:38.656380951Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-23T07:35:00.983605477Z","closed_at":"2026-03-23T07:35:00.982960426Z","close_reason":"Implemented 8 tree/forest: junction_tree, join_trees, random_unlabeled_tree/_rooted_tree/_rooted_forest, tree_data, tree_graph, complete_to_chordal_graph.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["generators"]}

Each algorithm now calls cgse_begin/cgse_record_decision/cgse_publish to emit ComplexityWitness records.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"franken_networkx-xv8j","depends_on_id":"franken_networkx-ckpt","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-09T00:24:41.484423285Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"franken_networkx-y1g","title":"Coloring & Planarity: equitable coloring, chromatic polynomial, embeddings","description":"## Scope
Implement 4 coloring and planarity functions:
- `equitable_color` — find equitable k-coloring (color classes differ by at most 1 in size)
- `chromatic_polynomial` — compute the chromatic polynomial of a graph
- `combinatorial_embedding_to_pos` — convert combinatorial planar embedding to coordinate positions
- `apply_matplotlib_colors` — utility to apply color scheme to matplotlib visualization

## Algorithmic Approach
- equitable_color: Use the Kierstead-Kostochka procedure. Start with a greedy coloring, then equalize class sizes by recoloring chains. O(V * k) where k is number of colors.
- chromatic_polynomial: Deletion-contraction recursion. P(G,k) = P(G-e,k) - P(G/e,k). NP-hard in general. Use memoization on graph isomorphism classes. For small graphs (<20 nodes) this is feasible. Can also use Whitney rank polynomial relationship.
- combinatorial_embedding_to_pos: Given face structure from planar embedding, use Tutte's barycentric method or Schnyder realizer to assign coordinates. O(V) for Tutte embedding.
- apply_matplotlib_colors: Simple utility, map color indices to matplotlib colormap values.

## Implementation Location
- Python: `python/franken_networkx/algorithms/coloring/` and `python/franken_networkx/algorithms/planarity.py`
- chromatic_polynomial could benefit from Rust for memoized recursion

## Edge Cases
- equitable_color: graph requiring more than Delta+1 colors (should raise)
- chromatic_polynomial: very slow for dense graphs > 15 nodes
- combinatorial_embedding_to_pos: non-planar input (should raise or handle gracefully)

## Test Requirements
- equitable_color: verify valid coloring AND equitability (class sizes differ by <= 1)
- chromatic_polynomial: verify P(G,k) = 0 for k < chi(G), P(K_n, k) = k(k-1)...(k-n+1)
- Cross-validate against NetworkX","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-21T19:14:02.971518597Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-23T07:35:00.719565439Z","closed_at":"2026-03-23T07:35:00.719053861Z","close_reason":"Implemented 3 coloring/planarity: equitable_color, chromatic_polynomial, combinatorial_embedding_to_pos.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["algorithms","coloring","planarity","tier-3"]}

Bipartite coverage improved from ~10% to ~30%.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

@@ -0,0 +1,336 @@
+{
+  "schema_version": "1.0.0",
+  "artifact_id": "cgse-adversarial-tiebreak-corpus-v1",
+  "generated_at_utc": "2026-04-10T02:30:00Z",
+  "description": "Adversarial test graphs that force tie-break decisions in all 12 CGSE reference algorithms. Each case is designed to trigger the algorithm's declared tie-break policy.",
+  "test_cases": [
+    {
+      "case_id": "dijkstra_equal_weight_paths",
+      "algorithm": "dijkstra",
+      "policy": "weight_then_lex",
+      "description": "Two paths from A to D with identical total weight; lex-min path should win",
+      "graph": {
+        "directed": false,
+        "nodes": ["a", "b", "c", "d"],
+        "edges": [
+          {"u": "a", "v": "b", "weight": 1.0},
+          {"u": "a", "v": "c", "weight": 1.0},
+          {"u": "b", "v": "d", "weight": 1.0},
+          {"u": "c", "v": "d", "weight": 1.0}
+        ]
+      },
+      "query": {"source": "a", "target": "d"},
+      "expected_path": ["a", "b", "d"],
+      "expected_witness_property": "deterministic_hash",
+      "adversarial_property": "equal_weight_tie"
+    },
+    {
+      "case_id": "dijkstra_three_way_tie",
+      "algorithm": "dijkstra",
+      "policy": "weight_then_lex",
+      "description": "Three equal-weight paths; lex-min intermediate node wins",
+      "graph": {
+        "directed": false,
+        "nodes": ["s", "a", "b", "c", "t"],
+        "edges": [
+          {"u": "s", "v": "a", "weight": 1.0},
+          {"u": "s", "v": "b", "weight": 1.0},
+          {"u": "s", "v": "c", "weight": 1.0},
+          {"u": "a", "v": "t", "weight": 1.0},
+          {"u": "b", "v": "t", "weight": 1.0},
+          {"u": "c", "v": "t", "weight": 1.0}
+        ]
+      },
+      "query": {"source": "s", "target": "t"},
+      "expected_path": ["s", "a", "t"],
+      "expected_witness_property": "deterministic_hash",
+      "adversarial_property": "three_way_weight_tie"
+    },
+    {
+      "case_id": "bellman_ford_equal_weight_relaxation",
+      "algorithm": "bellman_ford",
+      "policy": "insertion_order",
+      "description": "Multiple relaxation candidates with equal improvement",
+      "graph": {
+        "directed": false,
+        "nodes": ["s", "a", "b", "c", "t"],
+        "edges": [
+          {"u": "s", "v": "a", "weight": 2.0},
+          {"u": "s", "v": "b", "weight": 2.0},
+          {"u": "a", "v": "t", "weight": 1.0},
+          {"u": "b", "v": "t", "weight": 1.0},
+          {"u": "s", "v": "c", "weight": 1.0},
+          {"u": "c", "v": "t", "weight": 2.0}
+        ]
+      },
+      "query": {"source": "s", "target": "t"},
+      "expected_witness_property": "deterministic_hash",
+      "adversarial_property": "equal_relaxation_candidates"
+    },
+    {
+      "case_id": "bfs_equal_depth_neighbors",
+      "algorithm": "bfs",
+      "policy": "insertion_order",
+      "description": "Multiple neighbors at equal depth; insertion order determines visit sequence",
+      "graph": {
+        "directed": false,
+        "nodes": ["root", "a", "b", "c", "d", "e", "f"],
+        "edges": [
+          {"u": "root", "v": "c"},
+          {"u": "root", "v": "a"},
+          {"u": "root", "v": "b"},
+          {"u": "a", "v": "d"},
+          {"u": "b", "v": "e"},
+          {"u": "c", "v": "f"}
+        ]
+      },
+      "query": {"source": "root"},
+      "expected_witness_property": "deterministic_hash",
+      "adversarial_property": "sibling_order_sensitivity"
+    },
+    {
+      "case_id": "dfs_branching_tie",
+      "algorithm": "dfs",
+      "policy": "insertion_order",
+      "description": "Equal-depth branching nodes; insertion order determines DFS traversal",
+      "graph": {
+        "directed": false,
+        "nodes": ["root", "a", "b", "c", "leaf_a", "leaf_b", "leaf_c"],
+        "edges": [
+          {"u": "root", "v": "b"},
+          {"u": "root", "v": "c"},
+          {"u": "root", "v": "a"},
+          {"u": "a", "v": "leaf_a"},
+          {"u": "b", "v": "leaf_b"},
+          {"u": "c", "v": "leaf_c"}
+        ]
+      },
+      "query": {"source": "root"},
+      "expected_witness_property": "deterministic_hash",
+      "adversarial_property": "dfs_stack_order"
+    },
+    {
+      "case_id": "connected_components_symmetric",
+      "algorithm": "connected_components",
+      "policy": "lex_min",
+      "description": "Symmetric graph where component root selection matters",
+      "graph": {
+        "directed": false,
+        "nodes": ["x", "y", "z", "p", "q", "r"],
+        "edges": [
+          {"u": "x", "v": "y"},
+          {"u": "y", "v": "z"},
+          {"u": "p", "v": "q"},
+          {"u": "q", "v": "r"}
+        ]
+      },
+      "query": {},
+      "expected_components": [["p", "q", "r"], ["x", "y", "z"]],
+      "expected_witness_property": "deterministic_hash",
+      "adversarial_property": "component_root_selection"
